batches.name: VLSI Design & Architecture - 2026
batches.description: Specialized training on VLSI architecture, physical design, and verilog modeling.

batches.name: Embedded Systems & IoT - Batch A
batches.description: Focus on RTOS, ARM microcontroller programming, and semiconductor hardware design.

batches.name: RF Microelectronics & Testing
batches.description: Radio Frequency design, impedance matching, and high-frequency chip testing methodologies.

courses.name: VLSI Physical Design & Verilog
courses.description: Comprehensive introduction to VLSI layouts, physical design stages, floorplanning, CTS, routing, and Verilog HDL.
courses.syllabus:
Module 1: Introduction to VLSI
Module 2: Synthesis & Floorplanning
Module 3: Clock Tree Synthesis & Routing
Module 4: Physical Verification & DRC

courses.name: Advanced Photolithography & EUV
courses.description: In-depth study of nanometer scale chip fabrication, laser-produced tin plasma EUV light sources, and reflective optics.
courses.syllabus:
Module 1: EUV Light Systems
Module 2: High-NA EUV Optics
Module 3: Mask Defectivity

courses.name: Embedded Systems & Real-Time Kernels
courses.description: Deep dive into embedded ARM systems, RTOS kernel synchronization, task scheduling, and microcontrollers.
courses.syllabus:
Module 1: ARM Architecture
Module 2: FreeRTOS Task Scheduling
Module 3: Memory Management

courses.name: Semiconductor Fab Operations & Safety
courses.description: Cleanroom protocols, silicon ingot slicing, oxidation, wet/dry etching, chemical vapor deposition, and safety standards.
courses.syllabus:
Module 1: Cleanroom Air Filtration
Module 2: Etching and Doping Processes
Module 3: Hazard Management

job_postings.title: Silicon Verification Engineer
job_postings.description: Join the core design validation team. You will write UVM testbenches, run coverage-driven metrics, and verify sub-system blocks.
job_postings.requirements: Solid understanding of SystemVerilog, UVM, and assertion-based validation protocols. Experience with Synopsys VCS or Siemens Questa.
job_postings.location: Austin, TX / Hillsboro, OR / Bangalore, IN

job_postings.title: Physical Design & Layout Engineer
job_postings.description: Responsible for floorplanning, clock tree synthesis (CTS), routing, and DRC/LVS physical sign-off at advanced nodes.
job_postings.requirements: Hands-on experience with Cadence Innovus or Synopsys ICC2. Knowledge of RC parasitics extraction and electro-migration limits.
job_postings.location: San Jose, CA / Hsinchu, TW / Bangalore, IN

job_postings.title: EUV Lithography Process Specialist
job_postings.description: Manage lithographic exposure processes inside cleanroom fabs. Tune source parameters and optical pellicle alignments.
job_postings.requirements: PhD or Master's in Physics, Material Science, or Optical Engineering. Experience with high-precision optical equipment.

lessons.title: 1.1 Technical Intro & Scope Overview
lessons.content: Introduction to the core microarchitecture design challenges. This lesson covers key industry requirements, technology nodes (sub-5nm), and advanced logic library characterization. We will explore the theoretical boundaries of physical scaling.

lessons.title: 1.2 Theoretical Physics & Substrate Chemistry
lessons.content: Detailed documentation on silicon wafer crystal structures, epitaxial growth techniques, and dopant diffusion models. This write-up outlines the chemical equations governing high-K metal gates (HKMG) and shallow trench isolation (STI) boundaries.

lessons.title: 1.3 Live Seminar: Advanced Physical Modeling
lessons.content: Real-time interactive seminar covering multi-gate architectures (FinFETs, GAA nanosheets). The session includes a live whiteboard demonstration on gate pitch scaling and parasitics mitigation.

lessons.title: 1.4 Timing Analysis & Static Slack Calculation
lessons.content: Deep technical lecture on setup and hold constraints. Learn about timing paths, clock jitter, skew budgets, and how cell delay is calculated using Non-Linear Delay Models (NLDM) and Composite Current Source (CCS) models.

lessons.title: 2.1 Logic Synthesis & Constraint Mapping
lessons.content: This video lesson walks through translating RTL code (Verilog/SystemVerilog) into gate-level netlists. We focus on writing timing constraints (.sdc files), mapping to standard cell libraries, and optimizing for Area-Power-Performance trade-offs.

lessons.title: 2.2 Layout Placement & Floorplanning Labs
lessons.content: Laboratory guide for core placement. Includes guidelines for placing macros, defining power pads, establishing the routing grid, and debugging early congestion hotspots.

lessons.title: 2.3 Live Lab: Clock Tree Synthesis Walkthrough
lessons.content: Interactive live session focusing on clock tree routing. We will configure H-tree and mesh structures, analyze clock buffer insertion, and balance insertion delays to minimize dynamic skew.

quiz_questions.questionText: Which stage of the physical design flow is responsible for planning the power grid and placing I/O pads?
What type of light source is primarily used in modern High-NA EUV lithography systems?
ASIC stands for which of the following?
Clock Tree Synthesis (CTS) is performed to minimize which of the following parameters?
What is the primary function of cleanroom HEPA/ULPA filtration systems?

options.text: Regulate the chemical composition of photoresist
